skill.name: frontend-review
skill.description: Review frontend code for accessibility, UI security, missing states, and UX regressions. Use it for React component reviews, pre-merge checks, and unsafe rendering reviews.
skill.summary: Review frontend changes for accessibility, UI security, missing states, and UX regressions before merge.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography patterns and shell backtick execution, but review found no cryptographic code or command execution. The flagged lines are skill descriptions and Markdown inline code around a local checklist path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Skill Description
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as weak cryptography. The line is only frontmatter text describing accessibility, UI security, and UX review scope. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, hashing, encryption, or insecure algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain description text and contains no executable code. The surrounding file defines a review workflow, not cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Interface Metadata
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged agents/interface.yaml line 3 as weak cryptography. The line is only a short description for the skill interface. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, hashing, encryption, or insecure algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is YAML metadata with a user-facing description. It does not define or invoke any cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Around Local Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The backticks are Markdown formatting around references/checklist.md. No evidence found of shell execution, Ruby execution, dynamic command construction, or external process invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks format a local documentation path inside Markdown. There is no executable context and no command interpreter is invoked.

content.value_statement: Frontend reviews often miss accessibility gaps, unsafe rendering paths, and incomplete UI states. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a focused review checklist for practical pre-merge feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews React and frontend changes with a pre-merge mindset.
Checks accessibility semantics, keyboard navigation, focus handling, and contrast concerns.
Flags unsafe rendering paths and dangerous HTML injection risks.
Identifies missing loading, error, empty, disabled, and pending states.
Reports findings first, ordered by severity with concrete file references.
Separates confirmed issues from open questions and residual test gaps.
limitations: It depends on the code and context provided in the chat or workspace.
It does not run automated accessibility tests by itself.
It is focused on review guidance, not full frontend implementation.
It may need product requirements to judge intended user flows.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge React Review
use_cases.description: Review a pull request for accessibility, unsafe rendering, and missing user states before approval.

use_cases.title: Design System Component Check
use_cases.description: Assess shared components for keyboard behavior, focus management, disabled states, and consistent error handling.

use_cases.title: Security-Aware UI Review
use_cases.description: Find UI paths that render untrusted content or handle forms without clear validation and recovery states.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-review to review this component for accessibility, unsafe rendering, and missing loading or error states.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before merging a frontend pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-review on this frontend change. Prioritize findings by severity and include concrete file references.

prompt_templates.title: Form Flow Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for forms, checkout flows, or account settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-review to inspect this form flow for validation gaps, pending states, disabled states, focus handling, and error recovery.

prompt_templates.title: Security and Accessibility Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex or user-generated content surfaces.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-review for a strict review of unsafe rendering, dangerous HTML injection paths, keyboard navigation, focus behavior, and residual test gaps.

output_examples.input: Review a new modal component before merge.
output: Finding: The dialog opens without moving focus into the modal.
Impact: Keyboard users may continue interacting with background controls.
Fix: Move focus to the dialog title or first actionable control when opened.

output_examples.input: Review a search results page with async loading.
output: Finding: The empty state and error state are not distinct.
Impact: Users cannot tell whether no results exist or the request failed.
Fix: Add separate empty, loading, and retryable error states.

output_examples.input: Review a component that renders user-provided content.
output: Finding: The review should confirm whether rendered content is sanitized before display.
Impact: Untrusted markup can become a cross-site scripting risk.
Fix: Use safe rendering defaults and document any trusted HTML boundary.

best_practices: Provide the changed files, related components, and expected user flow.
Ask for findings first, then open questions and test gaps.
Include product requirements when accessibility or state behavior depends on intent.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for automated accessibility testing.
Do not ask for generic UX advice without code or user flow context.
Do not ignore open questions when requirements are missing.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews frontend code for accessibility, UI security, missing states, and UX regressions.

faq.question: Does it support React?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for React component reviews and general frontend changes.

faq.question: Can it find security issues?
faq.answer: It can flag unsafe rendering paths and dangerous HTML injection risks in UI code.

faq.question: Does it run tests?
faq.answer: No. It guides review output and can identify where tests or manual checks are missing.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Provide changed files, related UI flows, and any accessibility or product requirements.

faq.question: How should results be used?
faq.answer: Use the findings as pre-merge review feedback, then verify fixes with code review and tests.
